objectives: Collect and properly wear all required Personal Protective Equipment (PPE)
Understand the function of the shroud in preventing steel oxidation during transfer from ladle to tundish
Learn the importance of timely shroud changes to prevent reoxidation, flow disruption, breakouts, and inclusions
Perform pre-change safety checks by monitoring tonnage displays and confirming slag detection
Remove worn shroud using the shroud manipulator arm controls
Rotate and position the shroud manipulator arm for lancing operation
Observe lancing operation to clear solidified steel or debris
Install new shroud by positioning and locking it at the ladle inlet
Coordinate with control room to resume casting operations safely
procedures: Collect all job-specific PPE items: helmet, gloves, boots, jacket, aluminized suit, gold plated face shield, leg guard, skull cap
Observe the shroud position connecting the ladle slide gate to the tundish inlet and understand the steel flow path
Review consequences of worn shrouds including reoxidation, flow disruption, breakouts, and inclusions
Check tonnage remaining in ladle and tundish on the digital display board
Confirm slag detector has triggered and slide gate has closed automatically
Hold the shroud manipulator arm from the platform
Press the down button on the manipulator arm to lower the shroud
Press the clockwise button to rotate the shroud away from the ladle
Move the shroud to the right using the manipulator arm before lancing starts
Hold the manipulator arm in place during lancing operation
Wait for co-worker to complete lancing operation on the shroud
Hold the shroud manipulator arm to begin installation
Press the anti-clockwise button to rotate the shroud to upright position
Press the up button on the manipulator arm to move the shroud close to the ladle inlet
Press the lock button to secure the shroud in place
Use walkie-talkie to coordinate with control room for casting resumption
sopContext.constraints: All job-specific PPE must be collected and worn before beginning any work to protect against hazards such as sparks, heat, UV radiation, and electric shock. Shroud change can only be performed after the slag detector detects slag and the slide gate closes automatically. The user must hold the manipulator arm in place during the lancing operation. In case of damaged or worn out shroud, the shroud must be changed for a new one and will not be lanced. After every four to five heat cycles, the shroud must be changed. The shroud must be properly positioned and locked before casting operations can resume. Coordination with the control room via walkie-talkie is required before resuming casting operations.
